questions.prompt: Why enable 'Include administrators' on branch protection?

choices.text: Without it, admins bypass every protection rule — the rules apply to everyone except the people most likely to make 'just this once' exceptions. Whoever can bypass sets the actual norm. Including admins means the rules are real: even for emergency hotfixes, the process holds. Combined with 'no generous bypass lists' (only documented automation accounts in bypass), the team gets a real safety net that doesn't erode over time. Emergencies should go through a documented process, not become normalized exception.

questions.explanation: Without admin inclusion, admins become the carve-out and the rules don't really exist.

questions.prompt: Team renames the CI job from 'test' to 'tests' (with an s). Branch protection mysteriously no longer requires CI passing. Cause?

choices.text: Required status check names must match exactly what CI produces. Renaming the job from 'test' to 'tests' broke the match — the rule still requires a check named 'test' which never runs anymore; the new 'tests' check runs but isn't required. PRs can merge without CI passing. Fix: go to Settings → Branches → Branch protection rules → edit the rule → update the required check name. Audit required check names every time CI jobs are renamed; rename rules are not auto-updated. Same pitfall for splitting one job into multiple — must add all the new names.

questions.explanation: Required check names must match CI job names. Renaming jobs breaks protection silently — audit on rename.
